tagline: Work with Zendesk Support API streams and typed reads from the command line.

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Zendesk connector credential/account scope.

commands.summary: Review Zendesk direct-read operations from the official OAS
commands.notes: This bundle now exposes 282 typed, bounded JSON direct-read commands. ETL streams and binary downloads remain separate lanes.

commands.summary: Review Zendesk binary/file read candidates from the official OAS
commands.notes: Metadata inventory only. api_surface.json tracks 37 binary/file read candidates; #162 must define bounded size/path/download policy before execution.

commands.summary: Review Zendesk sensitive/admin write candidates from the official OAS
commands.source_cli_path: Zendesk OAS POST PUT PATCH operations
commands.risk: Zendesk mutation candidates remain blocked until typed reverse-ETL schemas, risk text, approval text, redaction, and policy gates are added.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL execution remains plan → preview → approval → execute.
commands.notes: Metadata inventory only. api_surface.json tracks 210 sensitive/admin reverse-ETL candidates.

commands.summary: Review Zendesk destructive write candidates from the official OAS
commands.risk: Zendesk DELETE operations are destructive and remain blocked until typed reverse-ETL schemas, risk text, approval text, redaction, and destructive confirmation are added.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL execution remains plan → preview → approval → execute with typed confirmation for destructive actions.
commands.notes: Metadata inventory only. api_surface.json tracks 85 destructive-action candidates.

commands.summary: Review deprecated Zendesk operations from the official OAS
commands.notes: Metadata inventory only. api_surface.json tracks 3 deprecated operation rows; #160 must confirm replacements or blockers.

examples: pm zendesk read get-sources-by-target --target_type sample --target_id sample --field_id sample --json

flags.name: authenticity_token
flags.summary: Legacy CSRF token. Ignored by API.

flags.name: subdomain
flags.summary: Specify the name of the subdomain you want to verify. The name can't contain underscores, hyphens, or spaces.

flags.name: include
flags.summary: A comma-separated list of sideloads to include. Supported values: `fields_metadata`.

commands.notes: Stream-backed Zendesk collection read generated from the official OAS.

examples: pm zendesk read list-approval-requests --filter-status sample --filter-assignee_user_id sample --filter-assignee_group_id sample --json

flags.name: filter-status
flags.summary: Filter by a comma-separated list of one or more approval statuses. Allowed values are active, approved, rejected, withdrawn. Maximum 100 values.

flags.name: filter-assignee_user_id
flags.summary: Filter by a comma-separated list of assigned user ids. Maximum 100 ids.

flags.name: filter-assignee_group_id
flags.summary: Filter by a comma-separated list of assigned group ids. Maximum 100 ids.

flags.name: before_cursor
flags.summary: Cursor for pagination. Fetch records before this cursor

flags.name: after_cursor
flags.summary: Cursor for pagination. Fetch records after this cursor

commands.notes: Stream-backed Zendesk collection read generated from the official OAS.

flags.name: filter-source_type
flags.summary: Filter audit logs by the source type. For example, user or rule

flags.name: filter-source_id
flags.summary: Filter audit logs by the source id. Requires `filter[source_type]` to also be set

flags.name: filter-actor_id
flags.summary: Filter audit logs by the actor id

flags.name: filter-ip_address
flags.summary: Filter audit logs by the ip address

flags.name: filter-created_at
flags.summary: Filter audit logs by the time of creation. When used, you must specify `filter[created_at]` twice in your request, first with the start time and again with an end time

flags.name: filter-action
flags.summary: Filter audit logs by the action

flags.name: sort_by
flags.summary: Offset pagination only. Sort audit logs. Default is `sort_by=created_at`

flags.name: sort_order
flags.summary: Offset pagination only. Sort audit logs. Default is `sort_order=desc`

flags.name: sort
flags.summary: Cursor pagination only. Sort audit logs. Default is `sort=-created_at`

flags.summary: Number of records to return per page.  Note: Default and maximum values vary by endpoint. Check endpoint-specific documentation for limits.  (Marked internal-only because only used with traditional offset pagination, ...

flags.name: assignable_from
flags.summary: Filters brands to only those assignable from the specified brand ID. A brand-separated brand is only assignable to itself, while account-separated brands are assignable to all other account-separated brands.

flags.name: include_deleted
flags.summary: When true, includes soft-deleted brands in the response.

flags.name: filter-ids
flags.summary: Optional comma-separated list of ids to filter records by. If one or more ids are specified, only matching records are returned. The ids must be unique and are case sensitive.

flags.name: filter-external_ids
flags.summary: Optional comma-separated list of external ids to filter records by. If one or more ids are specified, only matching records are returned. The ids must be unique and are case sensitive.

flags.name: sort
flags.summary: One of `id`, `updated_at`, `-id`, or `-updated_at`. The `-` denotes the sort will be descending.

flags.name: page-before
flags.summary: A [pagination cursor](/documentation/api-basics/pagination/paginating-through-lists-using-cursor-pagination) that tells the endpoint which page to start on. It should be a `meta.before_cursor` value from a previous re...

flags.summary: A [pagination cursor](/documentation/api-basics/pagination/paginating-through-lists-using-cursor-pagination) that tells the endpoint which page to start on. It should be a `meta.after_cursor` value from a previous req...

flags.name: page-size
flags.summary: Specifies how many records should be returned in the response. You can specify up to 100 records per page.

flags.name: ids
flags.summary: Comma-separated list of ticket field IDs to retrieve. Up to 100 values accepted.  Either `ids` or `keys` can be used, but not both.

flags.name: keys
flags.summary: Comma-separated list of ticket field keys to retrieve. Up to 100 values accepted.  Use field keys like 'priority', 'status', 'subject' instead of numeric IDs.  Either `ids` or `keys` can be used, but not both.

flags.name: creator
flags.summary: If true, includes creator information in the response.

flags.name: exclude_sub_selection_options
flags.summary: If true, excludes sub-selection options from dropdown fields in the response.

flags.name: ticket_form_id
flags.summary: Filter by ticket form ID.  Supports single ID or comma-separated list of IDs.

commands.notes: Stream-backed Zendesk collection read generated from the official OAS.

flags.name: ids
flags.summary: Ticket form status ids to retrieve records for

examples: pm zendesk read list-ticket-forms --active true --end_user_visible true --fallback_to_default true --json

flags.name: active
flags.summary: true returns active ticket forms; false returns inactive ticket forms. If not present, returns both

flags.name: end_user_visible
flags.summary: true returns ticket forms where `end_user_visible`; false returns ticket forms that are not end-user visible. If not present, returns both

flags.name: fallback_to_default
flags.summary: true returns the default ticket form when the criteria defined by the parameters results in a set without active and end-user visible ticket forms

flags.name: form_type
flags.summary: Filter ticket forms by type. Use 'standard' for regular ticket forms, 'service_catalog' for service catalog forms, or 'all' to return all form types

flags.name: associated_to_brand
flags.summary: true returns the ticket forms of the brand specified by the url's subdomain

flags.name: locale
flags.summary: Locale to use for the ticket form names. If not specified, the default locale is used.

flags.name: active
flags.summary: true returns active ticket forms; false returns inactive ticket forms. If not present, returns both

flags.name: end_user_visible
flags.summary: true returns ticket forms where `end_user_visible`; false returns ticket forms that are not end-user visible. If not present, returns both

flags.name: fallback_to_default
flags.summary: true returns the default ticket form when the criteria defined by the parameters results in a set without active and end-user visible ticket forms

flags.name: associated_to_brand
flags.summary: true returns the ticket forms of the brand specified by the url's subdomain

flags.name: external_id
flags.summary: Lists tickets by external id. External ids don't have to be unique for each ticket. As a result, the request may return multiple tickets with the same external id.

flags.name: start_time
flags.summary: Unix epoch time to filter tickets. Only tickets created or updated after this time are returned. Example: `?start_time=1332034771`

examples: pm zendesk read show-many-users --ids sample --external_ids sample --include_deleted true --json

flags.name: ids
flags.summary: Accepts a comma-separated list of up to 100 user ids.

flags.name: external_ids
flags.summary: Accepts a comma-separated list of up to 100 external ids.

flags.name: include_deleted
flags.summary: If true, returns inactive or deleted users.

flags.name: brand_id
flags.summary: When brand separation is enabled and `external_ids` is provided, scopes the lookup to users belonging to the specified brand. Only applicable when the account has brand separation enabled.  Accepted values:  * "all" —...

examples: pm zendesk read list-views --access sample --active true --group_id 123 --json

flags.name: access
flags.summary: Only views with given access. May be "personal", "shared", or "account"

flags.name: active
flags.summary: Only active views if true, inactive views if false

flags.name: group_id
flags.summary: Only views belonging to given group

flags.name: sort
flags.summary: The sort parameter used with cursor pagination. Defaults to "created_at". Prefix with '-' for descending order

flags.name: sort_by
flags.summary: The sort_by parameter used with offset pagination. Possible values are "alphabetical", "created_at", or "updated_at". Defaults to "position"

flags.name: sort_order
flags.summary: The sort_order parameter used with offset pagination. One of "asc" or "desc". Defaults to "asc" for alphabetical and position sort, "desc" for all others

commands.notes: Stream-backed Zendesk collection read generated from the official OAS.

examples: pm zendesk read list-tickets-from-view --view_id sample --sort_by sample --sort_order sample --json

flags.name: sort_by
flags.summary: Sort or group the tickets by a column in the [View columns](#view-columns) table. The `subject` and `submitter` columns are not supported

flags.name: sort_order
flags.summary: One of "asc" or "desc". Defaults to "asc" for alphabetical and position sort, "desc" for all others

examples: pm zendesk read list-active-views --access sample --group_id 123 --sort_by sample --json

flags.name: ids
flags.summary: List of view's ids separated by commas.

examples: pm zendesk read search-views --query sample --access sample --active true --json

flags.name: query
flags.summary: Query string used to find all views with matching title

flags.name: access
flags.summary: Filter views by access. May be "personal", "shared", or "account"

flags.name: active
flags.summary: Filter by active views if true or inactive views if false

flags.name: sort_by
flags.summary: Possible values are "alphabetical", "created_at", "updated_at", and "position". If unspecified, the views are sorted by relevance

flags.name: sort_order
flags.summary: One of "asc" or "desc". Defaults to "asc" for alphabetical and position sort, "desc" for all others

flags.name: include
flags.summary: A sideload to include in the response. See [Sideloads](#sideloads-3)

help_topics.name: zendesk-auth
help_topics.summary: Zendesk authentication uses OAuth bearer tokens or email/API-token Basic auth with secrets stored in credentials.

help_topics.name: zendesk-safety
help_topics.summary: Zendesk writes remain reverse-ETL gated: plan, preview, approval, execute, with destructive confirmation where required.

help_topics.name: zendesk-operation-ledger
help_topics.summary: The initial operation ledger blocks all official OAS operations until later lanes map exact executable surfaces.
